title: chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. cuneifolium
intro: Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. cuneifolium, also known as the Corn Marigold or Fleshy-leaved Pyrethrum, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family. This plant has been utilized in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum leucanthemum subsp. cuneifolium include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds and flu.
